reasoning_model_1: The model accurately reproduces the content of the script without any errors or repetitions. Regarding the paralinguistic feature '语速' (speech speed), the instruction required starting 'extremely fast' and gradually slowing down to 'slow'. The audio begins at a moderately brisk pace (00:00-00:03) but does not reach an 'extremely fast' rate. It transitions to a slower pace towards the end (00:04-00:07). While the direction of the speed change is correct (faster to slower) and the transition is smooth, the magnitude of the change is not sufficient to fully meet the 'extreme' requirement of the prompt. It sounds more like a natural, slightly emphatic reading than a deliberate stylistic speed manipulation.
reasoning_model_2: The model experiences a catastrophic failure in generation logic. Instead of reading the sentence once as instructed, it enters an infinite loop, repeating the target sentence approximately 20 times over a duration of 3 minutes. This violates the 'Content Accuracy' criterion significantly due to massive additions and the 'Fluency' criterion due to the unnatural mechanical repetition. While the individual iterations within the loop actually display a decent application of the speed change (starting fast and ending slow), the audio file as a whole is unusable and constitutes a severe hallucination/failure.
model_comparison: Model 1 produced a usable, natural-sounding audio that adhered to the text script, although its execution of the speed variation was conservative and lacked the requested intensity. Model 2 failed completely by generating a 3-minute loop of the same sentence. Therefore, Model 1 is the clear winner as it successfully completed the core generation task.
